name: fermatLastTheoremFor_of_five_le
text: `fermatLastTheoremFor_of_five_le : вҲҖ p, p.Prime вҶ’ 5 вүӨ p вҶ’ FermatLastTheoremFor p` (`Fermat/PrimeFive.lean`) вҖ” proven from:

name: FreyPackage
text: `FreyPackage` + `FreyPackage.of_not_FermatLastTheoremFor_p_ge_5` + `FreyPackage.fermatLastTheoremFor_p_ge_5` (`Fermat/FreyPackage.lean`, adapted from the FLT project, Apache 2.0) вҖ” a counterexample for prime `p вүҘ 5` normalizes to a Frey package (coprime, `a вүЎ 3 mod 4`, `b` even). Fully proven, no sorry

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve : WeierstrassCurve в„ҡ` with `IsElliptic` instance, `О”`, `bвӮӮ`, `bвӮ„`, `cвӮ„`, `j`, and `FreyCurve.j_valuation_of_bad_prime` (`Fermat/FreyCurve.lean`, adapted from the FLT project). Fully proven, no sorry

name: FreyPackage.mazur
text: `FreyPackage.mazur` (`Fermat/FLT/FreyCurve/Mazur.lean`) вҖ” the mod-p rep of the Frey curve is irreducible вҖ” now (2026-07-16) from two explicit nodes in `Fermat/FLT/FreyCurve/MazurTorsion.lean` (own work), following Serre (Duke 1987, В§4.1):

name: FreyPackage.exists_torsion_embedding_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.exists_torsion_embedding_of_not_isIrreducible` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the two nodes below: Serre's analysis produces full 2-torsion plus a rational point of order p on some curve; the `embedding_assembly` combines them into an injective в„Ө/2 Г— в„Ө/2p via CRT

name: FreyPackage.exists_two_torsion_and_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.exists_two_torsion_and_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the disjunction node below plus the Frey 2-torsion

name: FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible` вҖ” (2026-07-16): the Minkowski input is discharged by the node below

name: minkowski_character_trivial
text: `minkowski_character_trivial` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the subgroup form below: the kernel is an open normal subgroup containing every inertia image (`Subgroup.map_le_iff_le_comap` + `ker (ПҮвҲҳres) = comap res (ker ПҮ)`), hence everything, so ПҮ = 1

name: open_normal_subgroup_eq_top_of_inertia_le
text: `open_normal_subgroup_eq_top_of_inertia_le` вҖ” Minkowski, subgroup form: (2026-07-16) from the inertia dictionary below plus mathlib's discriminant theory (fixed field of the open normal kernel via the infinite Galois correspondence; finite Galois number field by `isOpen_iff_finite`/`normal_iff_isGalois`; `1 < finrank` from `H вү  вҠӨ`; `exists_not_isUnramifiedAt_int_of_isGalois` gives an everywhere-ramified prime `p`; going-up lifts it; the dictionary contradicts)

name: isUnramifiedAt_of_inertia_le_fixingSubgroup
text: `isUnramifiedAt_of_inertia_le_fixingSubgroup` вҖ” THE inertia dictionary: (2026-07-16, compiled in ONE shot from the pre-verified chain: `liesOver_span_iff` for `LiesOver`, residue-field finiteness via the fraction-ring bridge, `card_inertia_eq_ramificationIdxIn`, `ramificationIdxIn_eq_ramificationIdx`, `ramificationIdx_eq_one_iff.mp`)

name: inertia_eq_bot_of_le_fixingSubgroup
text: `inertia_eq_bot_of_le_fixingSubgroup` вҖ” the inertia transport (; whole chain now rests on the ONE local node below): the image of `localInertiaGroup q` fixing `L` pointwise trivializes `Q.inertia (L вүғвӮҗ[в„ҡ] L)`. SPLIT EXECUTED (2026-07-16): the transport is from `exists_prime_over_inertia_eq_bot_of_le_fixingSubgroup` (SOME prime above `q` has trivial inertia вҖ” 2026-07-16 via the VALUATION route, no decomposition groups and no henselian lifting: the chosen embedding `О№` carries `L` into the finite subextension `M = в„ҡ_q(О№ L)` of `в„ҡ_qбөғЛЎбөҚ`; `hle` + the `lift_map` put `M` inside the fixed field of the local inertia; the local node below then makes `q` a uniformizer of `рқ’Ә_M`; pulling `рқ”Ә_M` back along the ring hom `рқ“һ L вҶ’ рқ’Ә_M` induced by `О№` gives a prime `QвӮҖ вҲӢ q` with `e(QвӮҖ|q) = 1` вҖ” if `e вү  1` then `q вҲҲ QвӮҖВІ` (`ramificationIdx'_ne_one_iff`), so `q = qВІc` in `рқ’Ә_M`, making `q` a unit in a proper ideal вҖ” and `#I(QвӮҖ) = e = 1` closes via `card_inertia_eq_ramificationIdxIn`. Helper lemmas unconditionally: `asIdeal_toHeightOneSpectrumRingOfIntegersRat` (`v_q = span q` in `рқ“һ в„ҡ`) and `maximalIdeal_adicCompletionIntegers_eq_span` (`рқ”Ә(в„Ө_qЛҶ) = span q`, via `intValuation_singleton` + `maximalIdeal_eq_span_uniformizer`); rests on `maximalIdeal_map_eq_of_le_fixedField_localInertiaGroup` (`Deformations/RepresentationTheory/LocalInertiaFixedField.lean` вҖ” 2026-07-16/17, Neukirch II.9.11 from scratch: a finite subextension `M/KбөҘ` of `KбөҘбөғЛЎбөҚ` fixed pointwise by `localInertiaGroup v` has `e(M/KбөҘ) = 1`. Proof as planned: Galois closure `N/KбөҘ`; `|I(N/KбөҘ)| = e(N/KбөҘ)` at each finite level via `card_inertia_eq_ramificationIdxIn`, tower multiplicativity of `e`, finite-level inertia surjectivity by counting, and profinite compactness lifting of finite-level inertia elements to `localInertiaGroup v`) and `inertia_eq_bot_of_exists_prime_over` (conjugacy propagation вҖ” 2026-07-16: `exists_smul_eq_of_isGaloisGroup` gives transitivity on primes over `q`; the conjugation identity `g вҲҲ inertia (Пғ вҖў Q) вҶ” ПғвҒ»В№gПғ вҲҲ inertia Q` is a short definitional argument from `mem_inertia` + the pointwise-smul API `Ideal.IsPrime.smul`/`pointwise_smul_eq_comap`)

name: FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible_of_minkowski
text: `FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible_of_minkowski` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from the stable-line dichotomy leaf, the Galois descent for points, and the VГ©lu quotient leaf (all `MazurTorsion.lean`)

name: FreyPackage.stable_line_dichotomy_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.stable_line_dichotomy_of_not_isIrreducible` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from the semistability leaf below + the character bookkeeping: the stable line (`exists_stable_line_of_not_isIrreducible`) carries unit-valued characters (`exists_subCharacter`/`exists_quotCharacter`, the scalar-action-on-rank-1 argument `exists_unit_character_of_finrank_one`), with `det = ПҮвӮҒПҮвӮӮ` (`det_eq_subCharacter_mul_quotCharacter` via `LinearMap.det_eq_det_mul_det`) `= ПүМ„` (the det node + `cyclotomicCharacterModL_eq_toZMod`); kernels are open (they contain the open kernel of ПҒ, `isOpen_setOf_galoisRep_eq_one` + `Subgroup.isOpen_mono`); Minkowski (hmink) kills the everywhere-unramified character; `ПҮвӮҒ = 1` fixes a nonzero `wвӮҖ вҲҲ W` (a fixed point of exact order `p`), `ПҮвӮӮ = 1` trivializes the quotient action. SPELLING GOTCHAS (all resolved): (a) quotient triviality must be phrased via `W.mkQ`, not `ПҒ g v вҲ’ v вҲҲ W` (HSub instance search sticks); (b) `P.freyCurve`-instantiated nTorsion carries `Rat.commRing` vs the `Field.toCommRing` spelling baked into `galoisRep`'s codomain вҖ” defeq at DEFAULT transparency but NOT at instance transparency, so `letI`/`haveI` instances for the local spelling are invisible to TC search against the baked spelling; the cure is general-`V` lemmas whose instance binders are pinned by unification with the `ПҒbar` argument (pass `ПҒbar` FIRST, extra finiteness as a plain hypothesis, never an instance binder)

name: FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified
text: `FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified` вҖ” (2026-07-17): away from `{2, p}` the whole representation kills inertia (`FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified`, transported by the new generic-`K` bridge `character_localInertia_le_ker_of_isUnramifiedAt` + `Rat.subsingleton_ringHom`/`convert using 5` to reconcile the local-vs-generic `algebraMap` spellings вҖ” the local в„ҡ-spelling and `toLocal`'s generic one are NOT defeq-bridgeable because `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup.map` is unexposed; ring homs out of `в„ҡ` are unique, so propositional bridging works); the unipotent-scalar lemmas (`subCharacter_eq_one_of_sq_eq_zero`, `quotCharacter_eq_one_of_sq_eq_zero`, ) turn `(ПҒПғвҲ’1)ВІ = 0` into character-triviality

name: FreyPackage.inertia_two_unipotent
text: `FreyPackage.inertia_two_unipotent` вҖ” (2026-07-17): the Frey curve has multiplicative reduction at `2` (`freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_at_two`, ), and the pointwise Tate unipotence leaf below transports through `map_mem_inertiaSubgroup_of_mem_localInertiaGroup`, the `(AвҲ’1)ВІ = AВ·A вҲ’ A вҲ’ A + 1` End-expansion (pointwise via `abel`), and the show-cast `вҒ„`-ambient collapse. SPELLING NOTE: a direct `exact` across the generic-vs-`Rat` `algebraMap` spellings is impossible (unexposed `IsAlgClosed.lift`); the working recipe is atom-level `rfl`-bridges (`hb`) for the representation-vs-`Point.map` steps plus `convert hp using 8` with closers `rfl`, `Subsingleton.elim`, and `congrArg` of `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup.map` (hom-level AND `Пғ`-applied) over `Rat.subsingleton_ringHom`

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_unipotent_of_multiplicative_reduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.torsion_unipotent_of_multiplicative_reduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, stated 2026-07-17) вҖ” pointwise Tate unipotence: multiplicative reduction at `q вү  p` (`q = 2` allowed, no `p вҲЈ v(j)`) makes every inertia element at a valuation subring over `в„Ө_(q)` act with `Пғ(ПғP) вҲ’ ПғP вҲ’ ПғP + P = 0` on the `p`-torsion (to be closed against the Tate-uniformization leaves)

name: FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified_at_p
text: `FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified_at_p` (stated 2026-07-17) вҖ” flat/ordinary at `p`: one of the two characters is unramified at `p` itself (connected-Г©tale sequence in the ordinary/ multiplicative case; supersingular excluded by reducibility)

name: FreyPackage.exists_quotient_curve_point
text: `FreyPackage.exists_quotient_curve_point` (stated 2026-07-17) вҖ” the VГ©lu quotient leaf: a stable line with trivial quotient action produces `E'/в„ҡ` with full rational 2-torsion and a rational `p`-point (quotient by the rational subgroup; quantified over Weierstrass models)

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_point_eq_baseChange_of_fixed
text: `WeierstrassCurve.exists_point_eq_baseChange_of_fixed` вҖ” (2026-07-17): Galois descent for points: a point of `E(в„ҡМ„)` fixed by every `Пғ вҲҲ О“в„ҡ` is the base change of a rational point (`InfiniteGalois.mem_range_algebraMap_iff_fixed` on the coordinates + `baseChange_nonsingular` descent + `Point.map_injective` order transfer)

name: exists_stable_line_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `exists_stable_line_of_not_isIrreducible` (`Chebotarev.lean`, 2026-07-16) вҖ” a non-irreducible 2-dim mod-в„“ rep has a Galois-stable line

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_two_torsion_embedding
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve_two_torsion_embedding` вҖ” (2026-07-16): the Frey model has rational 2-torsion points (0, 0) and (aбө–/4, вҲ’aбө–/8) (the quadratic factors as (x вҲ’ aбө–/4)(x + bбө–/4)); they are distinct, of order 2 (fixed by negation `negY`), and generate an injective (в„Ө/2)ВІ вҶ’+ E(в„ҡ) via two `ZMod.lift`s and a coprod, with injectivity by the four-element case analysis

name: embedding_assembly
text: `embedding_assembly` ( 2026-07-16): in an abelian group, an injective (в„Ө/2)ВІ and an element of order exactly p (odd prime) assemble into an injective в„Ө/2 Г— в„Ө/2p (`ZMod.chineseRemainder`; the parts are separated by the coprime annihilators 2 and p)

name: WeierstrassCurve.mazur_torsion_bound
text: `WeierstrassCurve.mazur_torsion_bound` вҖ” Mazur's torsion theorem, weak form: no elliptic curve over в„ҡ has a subgroup of rational points вү… в„Ө/2 Г— в„Ө/2p for p вүҘ 5 (primality dropped as unneeded) вҖ” now (2026-07-16) from the faithful classification below: images of an injective hom from the finite group в„Ө/2 Г— в„Ө/2p are torsion (finite additive order), the hom corestricts into the torsion submodule, and 4p вүҘ 20 > 16 вүҘ the order of every group in Mazur's list (`Nat.card` comparison)

name: WeierstrassCurve.mazur_classification
text: `WeierstrassCurve.mazur_classification` вҖ” Mazur's torsion theorem, stated faithfully: the torsion submodule (`Submodule.torsion в„Ө E(в„ҡ)`) is вүғ+ to one of the fifteen groups в„Ө/n (n вҲҲ {1,вҖҰ,10,12}) or в„Ө/2 Г— в„Ө/2m (m вҲҲ {1,вҖҰ,4}). Mazur, Publ. Math. IHГүS 47 (1977); Invent. Math. 44 (1978)

name: FreyPackage.galoisRep_not_irreducible
text: `FreyPackage.galoisRep_not_irreducible` (B4, `Fermat/PrimeFive.lean`) вҖ” now (2026-07-16) from two explicit nodes, mirroring the FLT project's hardly-ramified plan (their B5/B6, stated in Lean here before upstream):

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isHardlyRamified
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isHardlyRamified` (`GaloisRepresentation/HardlyRamified/Frey.lean`) вҖ” now (2026-07-16) as the structure constructor applied to the four defining conditions, each an explicit node in `HardlyRamified/FreyConditions.lean` (own work):

name: FreyCurve.torsion_det
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_det` вҖ” det ПҒМ„ = mod-p cyclotomic character вҖ” now (2026-07-16) via the Weil pairing route (`EllipticCurve/WeilPairing.lean`, own work):

name: WeilPairing.exists_weilPairing
text: `WeilPairing.exists_weilPairing` вҖ” the Weil pairing: (2026-07-17) as the coordinate determinant form in a `finBasis` (`#E[p] = pВІ` вҹ№ rank 2), Galois-scaled by `det ПҒ` (`pairing_map_eq_det_smul`) = the cyclotomic character by the det node below

name: det_galoisRep_eq_cyclotomic
text: `det_galoisRep_eq_cyclotomic` вҖ” (2026-07-17): `det ПҒМ„` and `ПҮМ„` are continuous conjugation-invariant `ZMod p`-valued functions on `О“ в„ҡ` (continuity of `det вҲҳ ПҒ` from discreteness of `End` via `discreteTopology_moduleTopology`; `ПҮМ„`-continuity in `Chebotarev.lean`); they agree at `Frob_q` for almost all `q` (the leaf below + `cyclotomicCharacterModL_globalFrob`, ), and the Frobenius conjugacy classes are dense (`dense_conjClasses_globalFrob`, rooted in the Chebotarev node), so the closed agreement set is everything. Bridge `cyclotomicCharacterModL_eq_toZMod` (`ПҮМ„ = toZMod вҲҳ ПҮ`) via `modularCyclotomicCharacter.unique` + `toZMod_eq_ringEquivCongr_comp_toZModPow`

name: det_galoisRep_globalFrob
text: `det_galoisRep_globalFrob` (`EllipticCurve/WeilPairing.lean`, stated 2026-07-17): Frobenius determinant at good primes вҖ” away from a finite set of places, `det ПҒМ„(Frob_q) = q mod p` (the point-counting/Weil computation over the reduced curve; route: NOS reduction injectivity + Frobenius-isogeny degree). - (the other root of this derivation is the Chebotarev node `exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset`, listed under the ChebotarevвҖ“BrauerвҖ“Nesbitt cone.)

name: WeilPairing.pairing_map_eq_det_smul
text: `WeilPairing.pairing_map_eq_det_smul` + `WeilPairing.det_eq_of_conj` вҖ” : on a 2-dimensional space an alternating form transforms under any endomorphism by the determinant (basis + 2Г—2 computation), so scaling by `c` forces `det = c`

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified` вҖ” unramified outside {2, p}: (2026-07-16) by the case split `q вҲЈ abc` or not, from the two nodes below

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_good
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_good` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the two `FreyCurve/Semistable.lean` nodes below

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasGoodReduction_of_not_dvd
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasGoodReduction_of_not_dvd` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-16) вҖ” at odd `q вҲӨ abc` the Frey equation is `q`-integral (integrality via `freyCurveInt` + `FreyCurve.map`) with unit discriminant `(abc)^{2p}/2вҒё` (numerator and denominator prime to `q`, hence a unit of `в„Ө_(q)`; adic valuation `1` via `mker_valuation_eq_isUnitSubmonoid`), hence minimal (valuation `1` is maximal among integral models) with good reduction over `в„Ө_(q) = Localization.AtPrime`

name: WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasGoodReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasGoodReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) вҖ” the NOS local-global glue: good reduction at `q вү  p` gives `IsUnramifiedAt q` for the mod-`p` torsion rep, closed against the NOS leaf below via the embedded-valuation-subring machinery + spelling bridge (`map_mem_inertiaSubgroup_of_mem_localInertiaGroup`, `LocalInertiaFixedField.lean`)

name: torsion_unramified_of_good_reduction
text: `torsion_unramified_of_good_reduction` (`KnownIn1980s/EllipticCurves/GoodReduction.lean`): 2026-07-17, AXIOM-CLEAN вҖ” the NOS easy direction for odd primes `n` (hypotheses `(hp : n.Prime) (hodd : Odd n)` added and threaded through `isUnramifiedAt_of_hasGoodReduction` and `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_good`; Frey callers supply oddness from `P.hp5`). Proof: inertia fixes residues (`MonoidHom.mem_ker` + `toRingAut` + `ResidueField.residue_smul`); a torsion point `some x y h` has integral coordinates (`torsion_abscissa_mem`/`torsion_ordinate_mem`, Cassels); `ПғP` is again `n`-torsion with the same residues, so `torsion_abscissa_residue_ne` (separable reduction of `preОЁ'вӮҷ`) forces `Пғx = x` and `torsion_ordinate_eq_of_residue_eq` (the `ПҲвӮӮ`-BГ©zout y-quadratic argument) forces `Пғy = y`; `congr 1` closes the `Point.some` equality via `Affine.Point.map_some _ h`

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_multiplicative
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_multiplicative` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the arithmetic (`freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd` + `j_valuation_of_bad_prime`) and the Tate glue node below

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-16) вҖ” at odd `q вҲЈ abc` the equation is `q`-integral, `cвӮ„ = c^{2p} - (ab)^p` is prime to `q` (pairwise coprimality forces exactly one of `ab`, `c` divisible by `q`), so `v(cвӮ„) = 1` (minimality by the unit-`cвӮ„` KrausвҖ“Laska criterion) while `О” = (abc)^{2p}/2вҒё` lies in the maximal ideal

name: WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) вҖ” the Tate glue: multiplicative reduction at odd `q вү  p` with `p вҲЈ v_q(j)` вҹ№ `IsUnramifiedAt q`, by the same embedded-subring transport as the good case, against the new pure-Tate content leaf below

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat` вҖ” flat at p: (2026-07-16) by the case split `p вҲЈ abc` or not, from the two nodes below

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat_of_good
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat_of_good` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the arithmetic node `freyCurve_hasGoodReduction_of_not_dvd` (applied at `q := p`) and the flat glue node below

name: WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasGoodReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasGoodReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) вҖ” good reduction at `p` вҹ№ `IsFlatAt p` for the mod-`p` torsion rep, from the leaf below plus the shared flat transport `GaloisRep.isFlatAt_of_dvr_package` (see its own subtree entry under the multiplicative case)

name: torsion_flat_of_good_reduction
text: `torsion_flat_of_good_reduction` (`KnownIn1980s/EllipticCurves/Flat.lean`, 2026-07-16): good reduction over a DVR makes the `n`-torsion a finite flat group scheme (Hopf algebra, finite flat, Г©tale generic fibre, equivariant points isomorphism). Plus the division-polynomial node `isCoprime_ОҰ_ОЁSq` вҖ” restated for fields and directly (2026-07-17; the former `resultant_ОҰ_ОЁSq` node was DELETED, see the session-6 log)

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat_of_multiplicative
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat_of_multiplicative` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the arithmetic (`freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd` at `q := p` + `j_valuation_of_bad_prime`) and the glue node below

name: WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) вҖ” the peu-ramifiГ©e glue: multiplicative reduction at `p` with `p вҲЈ v_p(j)` вҹ№ `IsFlatAt p`, from the new content leaf below plus the shared flat transport

name: torsion_flat_of_multiplicative_reduction
text: `torsion_flat_of_multiplicative_reduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, stated 2026-07-17): multiplicative reduction over `в„Ө_(p)` with `p вҲЈ v_p(j)` produces a finite flat Hopf algebra over `в„Ө_(p)` (Г©tale generic fibre) whose `в„ҡМ„`-points are `О“ в„ҡ`-equivariantly the `p`-torsion вҖ” the peu-ramifiГ©e package in the same DVR-`вҲғ`-shape as the good-reduction leaf (Tate curve + Kummer theory content)

name: GaloisRep.isFlatAt_of_dvr_package
text: `GaloisRep.isFlatAt_of_dvr_package` (`Deformations/RepresentationTheory/FlatProlongation.lean`) вҖ” the shared flat transport, (2026-07-17): a DVR package over `в„Ө_(q)` with equivariant `WithConv`-points iso onto the rep's space (over a field `A`) gives `IsFlatAt q`. The open-ideal quantifier runs over the two ideals of `A`: `вҠӨ` via `hasFlatProlongationAt_of_subsingleton` ( вҖ” trivial Hopf algebra `рқ’ӘбөҘ`), `вҠҘ` via the transport `HasFlatProlongationAt.of_addEquiv` across `M вүғ+ (A в§ё вҠҘ) вҠ—[A] M` (`AlgEquiv.quotientBot` + `baseChange_tmul`)

name: GaloisRep.hasFlatProlongationAt_of_dvr_package
text: `GaloisRep.hasFlatProlongationAt_of_dvr_package` (same file) вҖ” the core points comparison, (2026-07-17): instantiation of the general-`K` core `hasFlatProlongationAt_of_hopf_package` at the proven arc `в„Ө_(q) вҶ’ в„ҡ вҶ’ KбөҘ` (`localizationToAdicCompletionIntegers`). The ENTIRE shared flat transport is now ; the two `IsFlatAt` glue nodes rest only on the two content leaves

name: hasFlatProlongationAt_of_hopf_package
text: `hasFlatProlongationAt_of_hopf_package` ( 2026-07-17): `G := рқ’ӘбөҘ вҠ—[R] H` (Hopf/flat/finite by base change; Г©tale generic fibre by `cancelBaseChange` twice + Г©tale base change), points by `dvrPointsEquiv` (`AlgHom.liftEquiv` three times + `algHomEquivOfFinite`), convolution and `О“ KбөҘ`-equivariance by the layer lemmas below

name: algHomEquivOfFinite
text: layer C `algHomEquivOfFinite` + `algebraicClosureMapAlgHom` + `mem_range_algebraicClosureMap_of_isIntegral` ( 2026-07-17): postcomposition with the embedding `О№ : в„ҡМ„ вҶ’ KбөҘбөғЛЎбөҚ` is a bijection on points of any finite `в„ҡ`-algebra

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isTameAtTwo
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isTameAtTwo` вҖ” (2026-07-16) from the arithmetic and the tame glue node below

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_at_two
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_at_two` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-16) вҖ” the Frey model is semistable at 2 by design: `cвӮ„ = c^{2p} - (ab)^p` is odd (`a вүЎ 3 mod 4`, `b` even force `c` odd), giving `v(cвӮ„) = 1` and KrausвҖ“Laska minimality; `О” = 2^{2p-8}(ab'c)^{2p}` (with `b = 2b'`) is in the maximal ideal since `2p > 8`

name: WeierstrassCurve.isTameAtTwo_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction
text: tame quotient at 2 from multiplicative reduction, now assembled by the split/nonsplit case split over the transferred reduction (`hasMultiplicativeReduction_padic`): the split half is the Tate exponent quotient, the nonsplit half is the leaf below.

name: GaloisRepresentation.not_isIrreducible_of_isHardlyRamified
text: B5 `GaloisRepresentation.not_isIrreducible_of_isHardlyRamified` (`GaloisRepresentation/HardlyRamified/Reducible.lean`, own work) вҖ” now (2026-07-16) from three explicit nodes in `HardlyRamified/Lift.lean` (own work), following Buzzard's 2026 EPSRC Lecture 4 (his B5a/B5b/B5c):

name: exists_hardlyRamifiedLift
text: B6a `exists_hardlyRamifiedLift` вҖ” an irreducible hardly ramified mod-в„“ rep (в„“ вүҘ 5) lifts to a hardly ramified в„“-adic rep over the integers `O` of a finite extension of `в„ҡ_в„“` (bundled in `structure HardlyRamifiedLift`: `O` + framed rep + reduction map + Frobenius-charpoly compatibility). Deformation theory / modularity lifting without residual modularity

name: residual_charFrob_eq
text: B6bc `residual_charFrob_eq` вҖ” the residual Frobenius charpolys of a liftable rep are those of `1 вҠ• ПҮМ„` (`XВІ вҲ’ (q+1)X + q`) вҖ” now (2026-07-16) from the faithful split ( from the FLT project's newer layer):

name: IsHardlyRamified.mem_isCompatible
text: B6b `IsHardlyRamified.mem_isCompatible` (`HardlyRamified/Family.lean`, ; conclusion named `IsInHardlyRamifiedFamily` as a marked ) вҖ” a hardly ramified в„“-adic rep lives in a compatible family (`GaloisRepFamily.lean`, defs, ) all of whose odd members are hardly ramified. STRENGTHENED (2026-07-16): the package now records injectivity of the coefficient-ring embeddings into `в„ҡМ„_p` вҖ” an audit of the glue's proof skeleton showed the upstream statement is too weak for the charpoly descent (algebraMap from a domain to a field need not be injective); true for the intended subrings of `в„ҡМ„_p`

name: residual_charFrob_eq_of_family
text: `residual_charFrob_eq_of_family` (own work, `Lift.lean`) вҖ” compatibility BOOKKEEPING вҖ” now (2026-07-16): extract the 3-adic member via the number-field embedding; its charpoly at Frob_q is `XВІ вҲ’ (1+q)X + q` by B6c's trace + the cyclotomic determinant at Frobenius + the 2-dim reconstruction (generalized to comm rings); transport through baseChange-conj to the family, descend to the coefficient field by injectivity of the embedding, ride compatibility to the в„“-adic member, descend to `O` by the strengthened-B6b injectivity, and reduce through `charFrob_compat`. Exceptional set: `SвӮҖ вҲӘ {2-place, 3-place}`. Consumes B6c and the в„“-adic Frobenius-value node. AUDIT RESTATEMENT (2026-07-16): the conclusion (and B6bc's, and the ChebotarevвҖ“BrauerвҖ“Nesbitt hypothesis) now carries a finite exceptional set `S` of places вҖ” the family's `isCompatible` only pins charpolys outside an unspecified finite set, so the `вҲҖ q вҲү {2,3,в„“}` form was unprovable; the density argument absorbs any finite `S` (new bridge: `toHeightOneSpectrumRingOfIntegersRat_injective`, distinct primes give distinct places, so a finite set of places excludes only finitely many primes in the auxiliary-prime selection). Proof ingredients consumed:

name: IsHardlyRamified.three_adic
text: B6c `IsHardlyRamified.three_adic` (`HardlyRamified/Threeadic.lean`, ) вҖ” a 3-adic hardly ramified rep has trace(Frob_q) = 1 + q for q вүҘ 5

name: IsHardlyRamified.mod_three
text: `IsHardlyRamified.mod_three` (`ModThree.lean`) вҖ” : a mod-3 hardly ramified rep has a О“в„ҡ-equivariant surjection onto the trivial character; B6c's eventual proof lifts this 3-adically. Children:

name: mod_three_reducible
text: `mod_three_reducible` вҖ” the Dickson/discriminant reducibility content (unchanged)

name: mod_three_of_stable_line
text: `mod_three_of_stable_line` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from the new local leaf below + the (now field-generic) character bookkeeping of `MazurTorsion.lean`: quotient character `ПҮвӮӮ` of the leaf's line, trivial-on-`ker ПҒ` (unipotent scalar lemma), open kernel (`isOpen_setOf_galoisRep_eq_one`), unramified outside `{2,3}` from `IsHardlyRamified.isUnramified` via the generic bridge + `Rat.subsingleton_ringHom` convert; `minkowski_character_trivial` (target-generalized to any group) kills `ПҮвӮӮ`; `ПҖ` := coordinate of the rank-1 quotient

name: exists_line_with_locally_unramified_quotCharacter
text: `exists_line_with_locally_unramified_quotCharacter` (`ModThree.lean`, stated 2026-07-17) вҖ” the LOCAL leaf: a reducible mod-3 hardly ramified rep has a stable line whose quotient character is unramified at `2` AND `3` (flat connected-Г©tale analysis at `3` incl. the Serre swap; tame quadratic condition at `2`). - NB the lift structure gained an `IsModuleTopology в„Ө_[в„“] O` field (statement strengthening of B6a's conclusion, true for integers of finite extensions of в„ҡ_в„“; required by B6b)

name: not_isIrreducible_of_charFrob_eq
text: `not_isIrreducible_of_charFrob_eq` вҖ” Chebotarev + BrauerвҖ“ Nesbitt вҖ” now (2026-07-16, `Chebotarev.lean` + proof in `Lift.lean`): the agreement set with `1 вҠ• ПҮМ„`'s charpolys is closed (module topology on `End` over `ZMod в„“` is discrete ; coefficient maps continuous) and contains the dense Frobenius conjugates, so BrauerвҖ“Nesbitt applies. Children:

name: dense_conjClasses_globalFrob
text: `dense_conjClasses_globalFrob` вҖ” Chebotarev density, topological form вҖ” now (2026-07-16) by the profinite limit argument (: cosets of fixing subgroups of finite subextensions are a neighborhood basis, `krullTopology_mem_nhds_one_iff`; the finite-level statement puts a Frobenius conjugate in every coset):

name: exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset
text: `exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset` вҖ” Chebotarev, finite level: for every finite subextension `E` of `KМ„/K` and every `Пғ`, the coset `ПғВ·Gal(KМ„/E)` contains a conjugate of a `globalFrob v` with `v вҲү S` (existence form of Chebotarev for the Galois closure of `E/K`)

name: not_isIrreducible_of_charpoly_eq
text: `not_isIrreducible_of_charpoly_eq` вҖ” BrauerвҖ“Nesbitt, 2-dim mod-в„“ instance вҖ” SORRY-FREE (2026-07-16): CayleyвҖ“Hamilton turns the charpoly hypothesis into `(ПҒg вҲ’ 1)(ПҒg вҲ’ ПҮМ„g) = 0`; on `H := ker ПҮМ„` every element is unipotent, Kolchin gives a nonzero `H`-fixed space, stable under О“ (H normal); if proper it refutes irreducibility (`not_isIrreducible_of_invariant_submodule`, via `Subrepresentation`); if everything, the image commutes (commutators die in H) and the common-eigenvector lemma yields an invariant line. Children (both proven):

name: BrauerNesbitt.exists_fixed_of_unipotent
text: `BrauerNesbitt.exists_fixed_of_unipotent` вҖ” Kolchin, 2-dim: a group of unipotent endomorphisms has a common nonzero fixed vector вҖ” (2026-07-16, ). Route: matrix helpers `trace_eq_zero_and_det_eq_zero_of_sq_eq_zero` (square-zero 2Г—2 has zero trace/det, entry computation) and `sandwich_of_det_eq_zero` (rank-one identity `NвӮҖNNвӮҖ = tr(NNвӮҖ)вҖўNвӮҖ`); unipotency of `ПҒ g`, `ПҒ gвӮҖ`, `ПҒ (g gвӮҖ)` forces `tr(NNвӮҖ) = 0`, so `nвӮҖ (ПҒ g вҲ’ 1) nвӮҖ = 0`; the line `range nвӮҖ = ker nвӮҖ` is preserved with square-zero scalar action, hence fixed pointwise

name: BrauerNesbitt.exists_common_eigenvector_of_commuting
text: `BrauerNesbitt.exists_common_eigenvector_of_commuting` вҖ” a commuting family annihilated by split quadratics on a 2-dim space has a common eigenvector вҖ” (2026-07-16, ): all-scalar case is trivial; otherwise a non-scalar member's eigenspace `ker (fвӮҖ вҲ’ a)` is nonzero (else `fвӮҖ вҲ’ b = 0` by injectivity), proper, hence 1-dimensional, preserved by commutativity, and its generator is the common eigenvector

name: cyclotomicCharacterModL_globalFrob
text: `cyclotomicCharacterModL_globalFrob` вҖ” ПҮМ„(Frob_q) = q for q вү  в„“ вҖ” now (2026-07-16) by mod-в„“ reduction (`cyclotomicCharacter.spec` at n = 1 + `modularCyclotomicCharacter.unique`) from:

name: cyclotomicCharacter_globalFrob
text: `cyclotomicCharacter_globalFrob` вҖ” the в„“-adic cyclotomic character evaluates to q at `globalFrob q` (q вү  в„“) вҖ” now (2026-07-16, the hardest assembly of the session): `lift_map` transports the action to `в„ҡ_qбөғЛЎбөҚ`; `в„“^k`-th roots of unity are integral (`IsIntegral.of_pow`); `apply_of_pow_eq_one` at the maximal ideal of the integral closure gives the q-power action (exponent = q by the residue node, side condition by the unit node); descend by injectivity of the chosen embedding (forcing the adic-completion algebra instance against the `ratAlgebra` diamond); conclude by `modularCyclotomicCharacter.unique` at every level and `PadicInt.ext_of_toZModPow`. Serves the glue at в„“ = 3 and ChebBN at в„“. Children: DERIVATION MAPPED (2026-07-16), all ingredients in-tree: (i) equivariance of `absoluteGaloisGroup.map` along the chosen embedding вҖ” ALREADY in the tree as `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup.lift_map` (`AbsoluteGaloisGroup.lean`), and `AlgebraicClosure.map` is definitionally `IsAlgClosed.lift` (`Deformations/Lemmas.lean`); (ii) `natCard_residue_quotient_toHeightOneSpectrum` вҖ” (2026-07-16): the contraction of the maximal ideal is the maximal ideal (`Ideal.IsMaximal.under` on the integral closure + local uniqueness); transport to `в„Ө_[q]` by `adicCompletionIntegers.padicIntEquiv` (maximal ideals correspond via `Ideal.comap_symm`), the `в„Ө_[p]` residue count is `p` (`toZMod` surjective with kernel `рқ”Ә`), and `natGenerator (q-place) = q` via `span_natGenerator` and the `ringOfIntegersEquiv` bridge. WITH THIS, `cyclotomicCharacter_globalFrob` and its mod-в„“ corollary are UNCONDITIONALLY ([propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]); the ChebotarevвҖ“BrauerвҖ“Nesbitt chain now rests on the single leaf `exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset`; (iiвҖІ) `isUnit_natCast_adicCompletionIntegers` вҖ” (2026-07-16): a valuation-subring unit is an element of valuation one; the completion's valuation restricts to the global one, which on integers is the `intValuation`, equal to one iff `p вҲү v` вҖ” i.e. `p вү  q` by `natCast_mem_toHeightOneSpectrum_iff`; (iii) `AlgHom.IsArithFrobAt.apply_of_pow_eq_one` ( `Frobenius.lean`): a Frobenius sends m-th roots of unity to their q-th powers when q вҲӨ m вҖ” apply at m = в„“^k via `isArithFrobAt_adicArithFrob`; (iv) transport through the embedding and conclude by `cyclotomicCharacter.unique`-mod-в„“^k plus `PadicInt.ext_of_toZModPow`

name: toZMod_eq_ringEquivCongr_comp_toZModPow
text: `toZMod_eq_ringEquivCongr_comp_toZModPow` вҖ” (kernel rigidity of ring homs into `ZMod p`)

name: n_torsion_finite
text: `n_torsion_finite` вҖ” (re-derived 2026-07-16, second route): the torsion count `card_torsionBy` is `nВІ > 0`, and positive `Nat.card` forces finiteness. Statement specialized to separably closed characteristic-zero fields вҖ” the only fields at which the tree uses it (`galoisRep` gained `[CharZero K]`). The former division-polynomial route (`TorsionFinite.lean` with nodes `eval_ОЁSq_eq_zero_of_smul_eq_zero`, `ОЁSq_ne_zero_of_charDvd`, covering arbitrary characteristic) is SUPERSEDED and removed вҖ” the frontier shrinks by two nodes

name: n_torsion_card
text: `n_torsion_card` (= nВІ over sep. closed fields, `(n : k) вү  0`) вҖ” now (2026-07-16, `TorsionCard.lean`, own work): `card_torsionBy` by strong induction peeling off the minimal prime factor вҖ” multiplication by `p := n.minFac` restricts to a surjection `E[n] вҶ’ E[n/p]` with kernel `E[p]`, so Lagrange + the first isomorphism theorem give `#E[n] = pВІВ·(n/p)ВІ`; no CRT needed. Faithful leaves:

name: TorsionCard.smul_surjective
text: `TorsionCard.smul_surjective` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from the three division-polynomial point nodes below: the fibre node gives a curve point `(xвӮҖ, yвӮҖ)` with `ОҰ n (xвӮҖ) = ОҫВ·ОЁSq n (xвӮҖ)`; `ОЁSq n (xвӮҖ) вү  0` by `isCoprime_ОҰ_ОЁSq` (BГ©zout, from the resultant node); the formula node computes `n вҖў (xвӮҖ, yвӮҖ)` with `x`-coordinate `Оҫ`; `Y_eq_of_X_eq` gives `y = О·` or `negY`, negating the preimage in the latter case

name: TorsionCard.smul_some_eq_zero_iff
text: `TorsionCard.smul_some_eq_zero_iff` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from `zsmul_some_aux` via the evaluation bridges (`ПҲвӮҷ(x,y)ВІ = ОЁSqвӮҷ(x)` on the curve) and the `ОЁSq_neg`/`neg_smul` reduction to positive `n`; the characteristic hypothesis dropped (not needed)

name: TorsionCard.exists_smul_some_eq
text: `TorsionCard.exists_smul_some_eq` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from `zsmul_some_aux` (negative `n` via negating the point, `ОҰ_neg`/`ОЁSq_neg`); characteristic hypothesis dropped

name: TorsionCard.zsmul_some_aux
text: `TorsionCard.zsmul_some_aux` вҖ” the multiplication formula (Washington Thm 3.6) вҖ” WIRED (2026-07-17): full strong induction `zsmul_some_aux_strong` (iff-strengthened, x-formula + ПҲвӮӮ-tracking package; generic case `zsmul_consec_step`, `ПҲвӮҳвӮӢвӮҒ = 0` case, collision cases, all closed) + the 2-torsion branch (`evalEval_ПҲ_of_even` + parity + `d = 2` Ward pattern). Rests on the four sharp nodes below

name: TorsionCard.evalEval_ПҲ_quadratic
text: `TorsionCard.evalEval_ПҲ_quadratic` вҖ” the `T(n,2)` elliptic-sequence relation on the curve `ПҲвӮҷвӮҠвӮӮПҲвӮҷвӮӢвӮӮ = ПҲвӮҷвӮҠвӮҒПҲвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒПҲвӮӮВІ - ПҲвӮғПҲвӮҷВІ` (pure specialisation of the universal `T`-family, no curve input)

name: TorsionCard.psi_tracking_prev_zero
text: `TorsionCard.psi_tracking_prev_zero` вҖ” degenerate tracking `ПҲвӮӮПҲвӮҷвҒҙ = ПҲвӮӮвӮҷ` at `ПҲвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒ = 0` (fixed-window consequence of the T-family + sum-companion; numerically verified)

name: TorsionCard.psi_tracking_prev2_zero
text: `TorsionCard.psi_tracking_prev2_zero` вҖ” degenerate tracking `ПҲвӮ„ПҲвӮҷвҒҙ = ПҲвӮӮвӮҷПҲвӮӮвҒҙ` at `ПҲвӮҷвӮӢвӮӮ = 0` (ditto)

name: TorsionCard.psi_eq_zero_iff_dvd
text: `TorsionCard.psi_eq_zero_iff_dvd` вҖ” the Ward rank-of-apparition pattern: minimal vanishing index `d` вҹ№ vanishing set is `dв„Ө`; `d = 2` instance seeded by `Res(ОЁвӮӮSq, ОЁвӮғ) = -О”ВІ` (where `IsElliptic` enters)

name: TorsionCard.evalEval_ПҲ_sum
text: `TorsionCard.evalEval_ПҲ_sum` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from the universal EDS route (`PsiSumCompanion`: anchor identity + coordinate-ring membership + domain cancellation over the universal curve + base change)

name: EllipticDivisibilitySequence.normEDS_sum_companion
text: `EllipticDivisibilitySequence.normEDS_sum_companion` вҖ” the universal `(вҳ…sвҖІ)` identity `bc(WвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒВІWвӮҷвӮҠвӮӮ + WвӮҷвӮӢвӮӮWвӮҷвӮҠвӮҒВІ) = WвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒWвӮҷWвӮҷвӮҠвӮҒ(db+bвҒө) вҲ’ WвӮҷВіbВіc` for `W = normEDS b c d` over any ring вҖ” pure EDS, no geometry; to be proven by Stange-style double parity descent through the two-parameter family `T(p,q)` (mathlib TODO `IsEllipticSequence normEDS`; descent certificates verified to exist, `scripts/eds/`)

name: TorsionCard.exists_point_x_smul
text: `TorsionCard.exists_point_x_smul` вҖ” rational points in multiplication fibres over a separably closed field (where separability of `[n]` enters)

name: TorsionCard.prime_torsion_card
text: `TorsionCard.prime_torsion_card` вҖ” (2026-07-17): the nonzero `p`-torsion is fibred over the roots of the division polynomial by the dictionary node; a shared counting skeleton (`key`) computes `#E[p] = 1 + mВ·deg g` for a separable `g` cutting out the torsion `x`-coordinates with uniform `y`-fibre size `m`. Odd `p`: `g = preОЁ' p` (separability node), `m = 2` (the `y`-fibre quadratic is separable since `ОЁвӮӮSq(xвӮҖ) вү  0` by the coprimality node), `1 + 2В·(pВІ-1)/2 = pВІ`. `p = 2`: `g = ОЁвӮӮSq` (separability node), `m = 1` (the `y`-quadratic has discriminant `ОЁвӮӮSq(xвӮҖ) = 0`, hence a unique root `-(aвӮҒxвӮҖ+aвӮғ)/2`), `1 + 3 = 4`. infrastructure: `yQuad` (the `y`-fibre quadratic), `derivative_yQuad_sq_sub` (the characteristic-free discriminant identity `(вҲӮQ)ВІ - 4Q = C (ОЁвӮӮSq xвӮҖ)`), `yQuad_separable` (explicit BГ©zout), `pointsAt` finset + biUnion counting

name: TorsionCard.separable_preОЁ'
text: `TorsionCard.separable_preОЁ'` вҖ” separability of the odd-`p` division polynomial (disc companion identity, `disc(ПҲвӮҡ) = ВұpбөғО”бөҮ`)

name: TorsionCard.isCoprime_ОЁвӮӮSq_preОЁ'
text: `TorsionCard.isCoprime_ОЁвӮӮSq_preОЁ'` вҖ” (2026-07-17) from the dictionary node, NOT from EDS strong divisibility: a common root `Оұ` over `kМ„` would carry a curve point `(Оұ, yвӮҖ)` that is `negY`-fixed (the discriminant identity `(вҲӮQ)ВІ - 4Q = C (ОЁвӮӮSq Оұ) = 0` forces `2yвӮҖ + aвӮҒОұ + aвӮғ = 0`, uniformly in the characteristic), hence `2`-torsion, and `p`-torsion by the dictionary вҖ” trivial by `gcd(2,p) = 1`, contradicting affineness. Route: `В¬coprime вҶ’ gcd non-unit вҶ’ root of the mapped gcd over `kМ„` (`IsAlgClosed.exists_root`) вҶ’ common root (`root_gcd_iff_root_left_right`)`, with the division polynomials transferred by `map_ОЁвӮӮSq`/`map_preОЁ'`

name: TorsionCard.separable_ОЁвӮӮSq
text: `TorsionCard.separable_ОЁвӮӮSq` вҖ” (2026-07-17): disc = 16О” вү  0 (`twoTorsionPolynomial_discr_ne_zero_of_isElliptic`), distinct roots over the algebraic closure (`Cubic.discr_ne_zero_iff_roots_nodup` + `nodup_roots_iff_of_splits`), separability descends along `Polynomial.separable_map`. Audits clean

name: WeierstrassCurve.galoisRep
text: `WeierstrassCurve.galoisRep` вҖ” CONSTRUCTED (2026-07-16). The formerly sorry-d DATA is now the genuine representation: the Galois action on points (`Point.map`, via the `DistribMulAction` instance) restricted to the `n`-torsion and made `ZMod n`-linear (`AddMonoidHom.toZModLinearMap`). Continuity: the coordinates of the (finitely many, via `n_torsion_finite`) torsion points generate a finite extension `F/K`; the rep kills the open subgroup `Gal(KбөғЛЎбөҚ/F)` (`fixingSubgroup_isOpen`), so every fiber is a union of open cosets вҖ” continuous into any topology on the target. `#print axioms`: sorryAx enters only through `n_torsion_finite`. Mazur/B4 are now statements about the REAL representation

name: group_theory_lemma
text: `group_theory_lemma` вҖ” (2026-07-16) in `Fermat/FLT/EllipticCurve/TorsionCounting.lean` (own work, not ): structure theorem for finite abelian groups + torsion counting in `ZMod m` (`#torsionBy d (ZMod m) = gcd d m`, via the first isomorphism theorem) + multiset determination (each prime `q вҲЈ n` occurs exactly `r` times, each exponent forced to `v_q(n)`) + CRT reassembly (`ZMod.equivPi`). Axioms: `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`

name: torsion_unipotent_of_split_multiplicative_adic
text: pointwise unipotence in the split case: the Tate uniformization witness feeds `tate_inertia_unipotent` at the local valuation subring, pulled back to `E(в„ҡМ„)` along the equivariant embedding; the remaining content is the base-change instance identification of the two `О©`-stage curve spellings.

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_tateEquivSepClosure
text: Tate's uniformisation over a separable closure, now DERIVED from the choice-free Tate-curve uniformisation and Tate's variable-change theorem: the variable change is `k`-rational, so its base-changed point equivalence is Galois-equivariant, and the equivariance transports through the composite.

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_variableChange_tateCurve
text: Tate's theorem (Silverman ATAEC V.5.3): a curve with split multiplicative reduction is a change of Weierstrass coordinates of the Tate curve of its Tate parameter.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_unipotent
text: local unipotence of inertia on Tate torsion, for ANY uniformization witness: the torsion class is represented by `u` with `u^p` a power of the Tate parameter; the parameter is base-field-fixed so `Пғ(u)/u` is a `p`-th root of unity; inertia fixes it (residue characteristic prime to `p`).

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_unipotent_of_nonsplit_multiplicative_adic
text: the nonsplit half of the unipotence statement, assembled from the LOCAL nonsplit node `tate_inertia_unipotent_of_nonsplit` by the proven `в„ҡМ„`-pullback glue (equivariant embedding + `Point.map` injectivity).

name: ValuationSubring.inertia_fixes_of_pow_eq
text: step (bвҖІ), the generalized root-fixing lemma: if `x^p` is a Пғ-fixed constant of the valuation subring with nonzero residue (and `p` prime to the residue characteristic), inertia fixes `x` вҖ” `X^p вҲ’ c` is separable over the residue field, so distinct roots have distinct residues.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_trivial
text: local TRIVIALITY of inertia on Tate torsion when the parameter is a `p`-th power up to units, for ANY uniformization witness: the torsion representative is recentred by a base-field unit power so that its `p`-th power is a Пғ-fixed unit constant of the valuation subring; `inertia_fixes_of_pow_eq` then fixes it outright.

name: valuation_eq_one_of_padicValRat_eq_zero
text: the reduced-fraction dictionary at `q`: a nonzero rational with vanishing `q`-adic `padicValRat` has `v_q`-adic valuation `1` (equal multiplicities on coprime numerator/denominator force both to vanish).

name: exists_unit_qUnit_mul_inv_pow_isUnit
text: the step-(d) witness: with `p вҲЈ v_q(j)`, the Tate parameter of the completed base change is a unit of the completed integers times the `p`-th power of the image of `q^m` вҖ” via `|q_E| = |j|вҒ»В№`, the reduced-fraction dictionary, and the `Valued`/canonical valuation equivalence.

name: torsion_trivial_of_split_multiplicative_adic
text: pointwise inertia-TRIVIALITY in the split case with `p вҲЈ v_q(j)`: the Tate uniformization witness feeds `tate_inertia_trivial` at the local valuation subring with the step-(d) witness, pulled back to `E(в„ҡМ„)` along the equivariant embedding.

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_trivial_of_nonsplit_multiplicative_adic
text: the nonsplit half of the triviality statement, assembled from the LOCAL nonsplit node `tate_inertia_trivial_of_nonsplit` by the proven `в„ҡМ„`-pullback glue; the `j`-hypothesis feeds through `map_j`.

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_trivial_of_multiplicative_reduction
text: pointwise inertia-triviality on torsion at multiplicative primes with `p вҲЈ v_q(j)` вҖ” the split/nonsplit case split; the local input to `isUnramifiedAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction`.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_unipotent_of_nonsplit
text: the LOCAL twist-transfer of nonsplit unipotence, now assembled: the enriched twist witness, the inertia-fixed embedding of the unramified quadratic extension, and the equivariant composite point equivalence transport `tate_inertia_unipotent` from the twisted minimal model.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_trivial_of_nonsplit
text: the LOCAL twist-transfer of nonsplit triviality, now assembled: as the unipotent analogue via `tate_inertia_trivial`, with the step-(d) witness applied to the twisted minimal model (same `j`-invariant through `variableChange_j` and `j_quadraticTwist`).

name: inertia_fixes_algHom_of_unramified_gen
text: inertia fixes every embedding of an unramified extension of `в„ҡ_qЛҶ` (generator with monic integral lift of separable residue reduction): the master root-fixing lemma applied to the image of the generator, extended by the adjoin-вҠӨ equalizer argument.

name: ValuationSubring.inertia_fixes_root_of_separable_residue
text: the master root-fixing lemma: inertia fixes any `A`-integral root of a polynomial over `A` with base-field coefficients and separable residue reduction (subsumes the `X^p вҲ’ 1` and `X^p вҲ’ c` steps).

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_tateCurveEquivSepClosure
text: the choice-free core of Tate's uniformisation: `E_q(О©) вү… О©ЛЈ/q^в„Ө` Galois-equivariantly ON THE NOSE, by the explicit series `X(u,q)`, `Y(u,q)` (whose Weierstrass equation is proven in `TateCurveConstruction`); glued over the finite subextensions of `О©`.

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_tateTorsionQuotient
text: the Tate valuation-exponent quotient on `p`-torsion, over any uniformization witness: a Galois-invariant surjection `E[p] вҶ’ в„Ө/p` by the exponent of the Tate parameter in the `p`-th power of any representative вҖ” the quotient of `0 вҶ’ Ој_p вҶ’ E[p] вҶ’ в„Ө/p вҶ’ 0`, carrying the trivial action in the split case. Step one of the tame-at-2 derivation.

name: hasMultiplicativeReduction_padic
text: multiplicative reduction transfers from в„Ө_(q) to the ring of integers of mathlib's в„ҡ_[q] (the tame-at-2 interface field): all valuation conversions are IsEquiv-chains through Rat.padicValuation and Padic.mulValuation; minimality is the unit-cвӮ„ KrausвҖ“Laska criterion.

name: exists_tame_quotient_of_split_padic_two
text: the split half of the tame-at-2 condition: the Tate valuation-exponent quotient of `exists_tateTorsionQuotient` transported to global torsion along the (bijective, by torsion counting) embedding into `в„ҡ_[2]`-torsion; the quotient carries the TRIVIAL local action, which is unramified and squares to 1.

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_tame_quotient_of_nonsplit_padic_two
text: the nonsplit half of the tame-at-2 condition, now ASSEMBLED: the exponent quotient of the twisted minimal model transports through the (ПҮ-twisted) composite point equivalence; Оҙ is the quadratic character of the unramified L as a continuous GaloisRep (locally constant on cosets of the open fixing subgroup of the embedded L), unramified by the Z2bar embedding-fixing leaf, squaring to 1 by Int.units_mul_self.

name: inertia_fixes_algHom_of_unramified_gen_padic_two
text: the Z2bar-spelling of inertia-fixes-unramified-embeddings, now PROVEN: spectral-norm membership dictionaries (mulValuation_le_one_iff, algebraMap_mem_Z2bar_iff), the decomposition/inertia bridges at Z2bar (spectralNorm_eq_of_equiv closure, Ideal.Quotient.eq), the local coefficient hom with norm-based unit descent, and the master root-fixing lemma.
